examples: Some forager ants go out, and as soon as they find food, they pick it up and come back to the nest.
The less food there is, the longer it takes the forager to find it and get back.
But the forager needs several contacts no more than ten seconds apart before it will go out .
The leaf-disks collected by the forager ants are transported back to the colony where the sap can be harvested for food.
The locally-minded duo keeps a forager on staff and raises Mangalista hogs with a collective in Mississippi (cochon means "pig" in French).
